title: ericameria × viscosa
intro: Ericameria × viscosa, also known as the greasewood or greasewood bush, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high resin content, which has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The resin of Ericameria × viscosa has been found to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential natural remedy for health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Ericameria × viscosa can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties in various forms.
